engineer-felicity-farseer.name: Felicity Farseer
engineer-felicity-farseer.html:
Felicity Farseer

#
Tier 1Ship
Location
System
Deciat
Body
6 A
Settlement
Farseer Inc
Meeting requirement
Exploration rank Scout or higher.
Modifications offered
  • G5 Frame Shift Drive
  • G3 Thrusters
  • G3 Sensors
  • G3 Detailed Surface Scanner
  • G1 Shield Booster
  • G1 FSD Interdictor
  • G1 Power Plant
Refers you to
Juri Ishmaak
Unlock hand-in
1× Meta Alloy (buy at Darnielle's Progress, Maia, ~80k CR).
Notes
A natural home base — Deciat sits close to the core systems and you'll return here constantly. Pin Increased Range to re-roll FSDs remotely at any workshop.
In-Game Description
She is a well-known explorer, although she is rumoured to have once been a deadly assassin before heading out into the black. Some claim she needed to 'cool off' after a high-profile case, so went exploring, but Elvira denies it. She is fascinated by all things alien and has become expert at spotting human fakes. She believes in being self-sufficient, and her tight-knit group grows all their own food.

engineer-elvira-martuuk.name: Elvira Martuuk
engineer-elvira-martuuk.html:
Elvira Martuuk

#
Tier 1Ship
Location
System
Khun
Body
5
Settlement
Long Sight Base
Meeting requirement
Reach a maximum distance of 300 ly from your career start.
Modifications offered
  • G5 Frame Shift Drive
  • G3 Shield Generator
  • G2 Thrusters
  • G1 Shield Cell Bank
Refers you to
Marco Qwent, Mel Brandon, Zacariah Nemo
Unlock hand-in
3× Soontill Relics (sold only at Garry's Reclamations, Cheranovsky City, Ngurii).
Notes
Don't over-invest materials here — she is a stepping stone, not a destination.
In-Game Description
She is a legendary explorer who engineered her own improvements to her Asp Explorer early in her career. She is willing to share some of that knowledge with fellow explorers and also operates an extensive training programme for technicians and mechanics heading out into the black. She based herself at her father's mining and refinery facility, though in fact her operation is separate.

engineer-the-dweller.name: The Dweller
engineer-the-dweller.html:
The Dweller

#
Tier 1Ship
Location
System
Wyrd
Body
A 2
Settlement
Black Hide
Meeting requirement
Deal with at least 5 different black markets.
Modifications offered
  • G5 Power Distributor
  • G4 Pulse Laser
  • G3 Burst Laser
  • G3 Beam Laser
Refers you to
Lei Cheung, Marsha Hicks
Unlock hand-in
Pay 500,000 CR (no commodity).
Notes
If you lack the 5-black-market prereq, buy 1t of anything in any Anarchy system and dump it on that station's black market, five times.
In-Game Description
A mysterious contact in the Aganippe underworld. No-one knows his background, and he and his small group of like-minded individuals closely guard his identity and that of their best customers. He is renowned for custom modifications for local criminal elements, and is known to help outsiders who have the right contacts.

engineer-tod-mcquinn.name: Tod "The Blaster" McQuinn
engineer-tod-mcquinn.html:
Tod "The Blaster" McQuinn

#
Tier 1Ship
Location
System
Wolf 397
Body
Trus Madi
Settlement
Trophy Camp
Meeting requirement
Earn more than 15 bounty vouchers.
Modifications offered
  • G5 Multi-Cannon
  • G5 Rail Gun
  • G3 Fragment Cannon
  • G2 Cannon
Refers you to
Selene Jean, Petra Olmanova
Unlock hand-in
100,000 CR worth of bounty vouchers (any faction).
Notes
30 minutes of bounty hunting at any RES near a Nav Beacon clears both the meeting and unlock requirements at once.
In-Game Description
He earned his nickname from a successful season in the CQC Arena. Since then he's garnered further fame as a bounty hunter, flying a Fer-de-Lance with seriously overpowered multi-cannons. He's always happy to help fellow bounty hunters. For his current venture he's teamed up with some old CQC friends to form a custom modification enterprise.

engineer-liz-ryder.name: Liz Ryder
engineer-liz-ryder.html:
Liz Ryder

#
Tier 1Ship
Location
System
Eurybia
Body
Makalu
Settlement
Demolition Unlimited
Meeting requirement
Cordial or Friendly with the Eurybia Blue Mafia.
Modifications offered
  • G5 Seeker Missile Rack
  • G5 Torpedo Pylon
  • G5 Advanced Missile Rack
  • G3 Mine Launcher
  • G1 Hull Reinforcement Package
  • G1 Bulkheads
Refers you to
Hera Tani, Etienne Dorn
Unlock hand-in
200× Landmines (buy at Eurybia, Lave, or Diaguandri).
Notes
Run Blue Mafia missions out of Eurybia to reach Cordial reputation, then turn in the Engineer Invitation Contract.
In-Game Description
A demolitions expert who used to work for Devastator and Sons, but left to work for a crime syndicate known as the Blue Mafia. Some claim she had a personal relationship with a member of the syndicate and was forced to leave when the connection was discovered, starting Demolition Unlimited. The syndicate quickly saw the merits of her ability and helped grow her team into something much bigger.

engineer-juri-ishmaak.name: Juri Ishmaak
engineer-juri-ishmaak.html:
Juri Ishmaak

#
Tier 2Ship
referred by Felicity Farseer
Location
System
Giryak
Body
2 A
Settlement
Pater's Memorial
Meeting requirement
Claim more than 50 Federal Combat Bonds.
Modifications offered
  • G5 Mine Launcher
  • G5 Sensors
  • G5 Detailed Surface Scanner
  • G3 Torpedo Pylon
  • G3 Seeker Missile Rack
  • G3 Frame Shift Wake Scanner
  • G3 Kill Warrant Scanner
  • G3 Manifest Scanner
  • G3 Advanced Missile Rack
Refers you to
The Sarge, Colonel Bris Dekker
Unlock hand-in
100,000–1,000,000 CR worth of Federal Combat Bonds.
Notes
Combat Bonds come from Federal Conflict Zones, not bounties — bring a combat ship.
In-Game Description
An ambitious combat specialist whose father was a pilot in a mercenary wing. He followed in his father's footsteps operating with the wing, and believes in careful preparation before any combat. After retiring from combat he set up a base of operations to focus on his passion for engineering.

engineer-selene-jean.name: Selene Jean
engineer-selene-jean.html:
Selene Jean

#
Tier 2Ship
referred by Tod McQuinn
Location
System
Kuk
Body
B 3
Settlement
Prospector's Rest
Meeting requirement
Mine at least 500 tons of ore.
Modifications offered
  • G5 Hull Reinforcement Package
  • G5 Bulkheads
Refers you to
Didi Vatermann, Bill Turner
Unlock hand-in
10× Painite (core-mine it; the Borann runs count toward the 500t).
Notes
Requires the Tod McQuinn referral first. The Type-11 mining vessel is the natural ship for the 500t / Painite grind.
In-Game Description
A former miner, Selene has a talent for improving a ship's hull defence, although she only offers her services to fellow prospectors. She's rumoured to be the daughter of a rock hermit, but rarely talks about her childhood. As a counterpoint to her secluded upbringing she has gathered a robust, trusted team, mostly fellow ex-prospectors.

engineer-hera-tani.name: Hera Tani
engineer-hera-tani.html:
Hera Tani

#
Tier 2Ship
referred by Liz Ryder
Location
System
Kuwemaki
Body
A 3 A
Settlement
The Jet's Hole
Meeting requirement
Empire rank Outsider or higher (one Imperial mission).
Modifications offered
  • G5 Power Plant
  • G5 Detailed Surface Scanner
  • G3 Sensors
  • G3 Power Distributor
Refers you to
Broo Tarquin, Tiana Fortune
Unlock hand-in
50× Kamitra Cigars (buy at Hammel Terminal, Kamitra — Imperial space).
Notes
Overcharged needs Selenium at G3 — trade for it at a Raw trader (no direct farm; 6:1 from capped G4s). You already hold the Kamitra Cigars.
In-Game Description
Her talent with power-management systems was evident from an early age. She grew up with the Jet Rats gang, keeping in close communication with them, but will also help those friendly with the gang. In turn they assigned anyone with sufficient technical ability to work and learn from her. Since their demise she has found favour with a mysterious imperial benefactor.

engineer-lei-cheung.name: Lei Cheung
engineer-lei-cheung.html:
Lei Cheung

#
Tier 2Ship
referred by The Dweller
Location
System
Laksak
Body
A 1
Settlement
Trader's Rest
Meeting requirement
Trade in over 50 different markets.
Modifications offered
  • G5 Shield Generator
  • G5 Sensors
  • G5 Detailed Surface Scanner
  • G3 Shield Booster
Refers you to
Ram Tah
Unlock hand-in
200× Gold (buy from any high-tech / industrial market).
In-Game Description
He grew up in a trade clan of tramp traders, learning engineering on the job and discovering an affinity for shield technology. Even though he receives regular offers, he won't leave his family. He is willing to help fellow traders and has built a large operation over the years, with other family members running a scrapping and rebuilding business on the same site.

engineer-marco-qwent.name: Marco Qwent
engineer-marco-qwent.html:
Marco Qwent

#
Tier 2ShipPermit
referred by Elvira Martuuk
Location
System
Sirius [permit]
Body
Lucifer
Settlement
Qwent Research Base
Meeting requirement
Invitation from the Sirius Corporation (Sirius permit).
Modifications offered
  • G4 Power Plant
  • G3 Power Distributor
Refers you to
Professor Palin, Lori Jameson
Unlock hand-in
25× Modular Terminals (mission rewards only — not buyable).
Notes
Get Allied with Sirius Corp via missions, take the permit mission, then wait for the invite mission to spawn (try O'Brien Vision). Modular Terminals farm best from passenger missions in High-Tech / Boom systems. Note: courting Uma Laszlo later requires LOWERING Sirius rep, so don't over-invest.
In-Game Description
An ex Sirius Corp. tech guru. Such is his technical talent that he's allowed some latitude with what he works on and who he works with, as long as Sirius Corp receive the appropriate recognition. They support his efforts by maintaining a significant base for him to operate from.

engineer-zacariah-nemo.name: Zacariah Nemo
engineer-zacariah-nemo.html:
Zacariah Nemo

#
Tier 2Ship
referred by Elvira Martuuk
Location
System
Yoru
Body
4
Settlement
Nemo Cyber Party Base
Meeting requirement
Invitation from the Party of Yoru.
Modifications offered
  • G5 Fragment Cannon
  • G3 Multi-Cannon
  • G2 Plasma Accelerator
Refers you to
— none —
Unlock hand-in
25× Xihe Companions (buy at Xihe markets).
In-Game Description
Zacariah has been at the centre of a small cult-like commune for decades, the Nemo Cyber Party. Rumour has it he has a cyber-enhanced brain that can take vast information dumps directly to it. His skill with weapons manufacturing is made available to those favoured by the Party, and he has attracted a growing group of 'cyber-freaks' usually shunned in normal society.

engineer-the-sarge.name: The Sarge
engineer-the-sarge.html:
The Sarge

#
Tier 3Ship
referred by Juri Ishmaak
Location
System
Beta-3 Tucani
Body
2 B A
Settlement
The Beach
Meeting requirement
Federal Navy rank Midshipman or higher.
Modifications offered
  • G5 Collector Limpet Controller
  • G5 Fuel Transfer Limpet Controller
  • G5 Hatch Breaker Limpet Controller
  • G5 Prospector Limpet Controller
  • G5 Cannon
  • G3 Rail Gun
Refers you to
— none —
Unlock hand-in
50× Aberrant Shield Pattern Analysis (encoded — scan ships in combat, or trade up).
In-Game Description
No one knows The Sarge's real name and he won't reveal it, even to close friends. He's an ex-Federal Navy specialist who worked closely with the now-defunct Colonial Drones Corporation, and later with the drone division of Core Dynamics, tweaking drone controllers and long-range patrol support systems. His reputation has attracted followers who learn from him.

engineer-colonel-bris-dekker.name: Colonel Bris Dekker
engineer-colonel-bris-dekker.html:
Colonel Bris Dekker

#
Tier 3ShipPermit
referred by Juri Ishmaak
Location
System
Sol [permit]
Body
Iapetus
Settlement
Dekker's Yard
Meeting requirement
Federation Friendly + Sol permit (from the Petty Officer grind).
Modifications offered
  • G4 FSD Interdictor
  • G3 Frame Shift Drive
Refers you to
— none —
Unlock hand-in
1–10M CR worth of Federal Combat Bonds (bank ~10M to be safe).
Notes
The Sol permit drops out of the Federal Navy Petty Officer rank grind.
In-Game Description
Colonel Bris Dekker retired with full honours a decade ago. He served as a Federal Navy officer in an elite recon squadron and has an almost natural affinity for frame shift technology. Even in retirement the navy call on him to evaluate changes or upgrades, and his facility has been vastly upgraded to support the training courses he provides.

engineer-didi-vatermann.name: Didi Vatermann
engineer-didi-vatermann.html:
Didi Vatermann

#
Tier 3Ship
referred by Selene Jean
Location
System
Leesti
Body
1 A
Settlement
Vatermann LLC

Trade rank Merchant or higher.
Modifications offered
  • G5 Shield Booster
  • G3 Shield Generator
Refers you to
— none —
Unlock hand-in
50× Lavian Brandy (buy in the Lave system; illegal in some Federal space).
Notes
Merchant rank comes naturally from the old Robigo runs — you likely already have it.
In-Game Description
Didi's father died several decades ago as a result of a shield-booster malfunction. Since then she has dedicated her life to making shield boosters safer. In recent years her success has been such that she now lives in luxury, with a team of dedicated mechanics helping to implement her designs.

engineer-bill-turner.name: Bill Turner
engineer-bill-turner.html:
Bill Turner

referred by Selene Jean

In-Game Description
Bill Turner claims to be the son of the famous but elusive late Mic Turner, and despite his young age he's reputed to be a wizard with utilities and plasma accelerators.

engineer-broo-tarquin.name: Broo Tarquin
engineer-broo-tarquin.html:
Broo Tarquin

#
Tier 3Ship
referred by Hera Tani
Location
System
Muang
Body
5 A
Settlement
Broo's Legacy

  • G5 Pulse Laser
  • G5 Beam Laser
Refers you to
— none —
Unlock hand-in
50× Fujin Tea (buy at Fujin, Imperial space).
Notes
Competent is ~3–4 hours of bounty hunting if you're not there yet — and it's on the way to the Corvette's combat rank.
In-Game Description
Broo Tarquin was the founder of Dead-Eye Defence Systems, now part of Thule Industries. Since retiring from his role as chief designer, he spends his days tinkering in his workshop and drinking exotic tea. Others have joined him in his work, and his workshop has expanded into a wider operation, making components from their own smelted alloys.

engineer-tiana-fortune.name: Tiana Fortune
engineer-tiana-fortune.html:
Tiana Fortune

#
Tier 3ShipPermit
referred by Hera Tani
Location
System
Achenar [permit]
Body
4 A
Settlement
Fortune's Loss

Empire Friendly + Achenar permit (from the Imperial Navy grind).

50× Decoded Emission Data (encoded — scan ship wakes, or trade up).
Notes
The Achenar permit drops out of the Imperial Navy Squire rank grind.
In-Game Description
An electronic-engineering genius rumoured to be the illegitimate child of an Imperial Guard officer. Her own military career was cut short when the circumstances of her birth were revealed, resulting in her expulsion from the guard. Many in her unit demonstrated their loyalty by joining her once they'd completed their service.

engineer-ram-tah.name: Ram Tah
engineer-ram-tah.html:
Ram Tah

referred by Lei Cheung

Meene

Notes
The Booster itself is a tech-broker module build at a Guardian site, not a graded blueprint.
In-Game Description
Ram Tah has travelled as far as it is possible to go in the galaxy. Over the years he has collected many strange and unusual items, and is always eager to acquire something new and unknown from the black. On his travels he learned the value of limpets and various defence mechanisms, and spent many years perfecting his craft in improving them.

engineer-professor-palin.name: Professor Palin
engineer-professor-palin.html:
Professor Palin

#
Tier 3Ship
referred by Marco Qwent
Location
System
Arque
Body
4 E
Settlement
Abel Laboratory
Meeting requirement
Reach a maximum distance of 5,000 ly from your career start.
Modifications offered
  • G5 Thrusters
  • G3 Frame Shift Drive
Refers you to
— none —
Unlock hand-in
25× Sensor Fragments (Pleiades Thargoid wreckage — grabbed in Phase 0).
Notes
The 5,000 ly meeting requirement is cleared trivially by the Colonia expedition.
In-Game Description
Professor Palin spent the early part of his career in the Federation, but in 3301 decided to establish an independent institute dedicated to studying the Thargoids. The following year he set up a research base focused on developing new defence technologies. He continues to research the Thargoids in Arque, and is considered one of the galaxy's foremost experts on Thargoid technology.

engineer-lori-jameson.name: Lori Jameson
engineer-lori-jameson.html:
Lori Jameson

referred by Marco Qwent

System

A 1
Settlement
Jameson Base

Combat rank Dangerous + Shinrarta Dezhra permit (Elite in any career).

  • G5 Detailed Surface Scanner
  • G4 Refinery
  • G4 Fuel Scoop
  • G4 Auto Field-Maintenance Unit

  • G3 Frame Shift Wake Scanner

  • G3 Manifest Scanner
  • G3 Shield Cell Bank

25× Kongga Ale (buy at Kongga).
In-Game Description
One of the great-granddaughters of John Jameson, the most famous Pilots Federation member ever. She is an accomplished explorer in her own right and has developed various techniques for improved automated systems. Her facility is popular with those preparing ships for extended deep-space operations, and she ensures pleasant surroundings while they await their upgrades.

engineer-chloe-sedesi.name: Chloe Sedesi
engineer-chloe-sedesi.html:
Chloe Sedesi

#
Tier 3Ship
referred by Marco Qwent
Location
System
Shenve (Witch Head)
Body
A 6

Cinder Dock

— none —

25× Sensor Fragments (second Pleiades batch).
Notes
Effectively a duplicate of Professor Palin — the same grade-5 Thrusters (Dirty Drive) line. Reached via a Marco Qwent referral; generally skipped unless a second thruster engineer is wanted.
In-Game Description
Chloe Sedesi served a number of artificer apprenticeships before joining Professor Ishmael Palin's staff as a mechanic. She swiftly became a vital team member, displaying remarkable aptitude across technical disciplines. When the Witch Head enclave was established, she resigned from Palin's team and, with his blessing, travelled to Cinder Dock to offer the techniques she had learned at the Palin Research Institute.

engineer-petra-olmanova.name: Petra Olmanova

Petra Olmanova

Petra Olmanova was a specialist combat technician believed killed in an explosion at Consolidated Plasma Corp's primary research facility. She survived relatively unscathed and resurfaced some time later as a freelancer. Her base has since become a sanctuary, and she spurns social interaction unless business is involved.

engineer-mel-brandon.name: Mel Brandon
engineer-mel-brandon.html:
Mel Brandon

100,000 CR worth of bounty vouchers.
In-Game Description
A specialist in laser-based weaponry, shields, frame shift drives and engines. He was dishonourably discharged from the Federal Defence Force for disobeying a senior officer who ordered a combat strike on a labour protest, but he was popular in the service and retains useful contacts. Many of his comrades-in-arms have since joined him in his business.

engineer-etienne-dorn.name: Etienne Dorn
engineer-etienne-dorn.html:
Etienne Dorn

Etienne Dorn used to be the lead shield engineer for Kraken Shield Specialists, but these days he's mostly self-employed. He still shares developments with his former employers, but usually offers products to private clients first — for the right price. His success has let him develop state-of-the-art facilities, but he prefers to work with robots to maintain his seclusion.

engineer-marsha-hicks.name: Marsha Hicks

Marsha Hicks has been in and out of more penitentiaries than she can count. She decided to go straight and move out to Colonia after the death of her daughter, when the Thargoids attacked Bhal. She doesn't like dealing with people, but everyone has to make a living, and working as an engineer is better than going back to jail.

engineer-domino-green.name: Domino Green
engineer-domino-green.html:
Domino Green

#

Body

5× Push (illicit good).
In-Game Description
Orphaned at the age of seven, Domino was placed in a public-relations initiative by the Rockforth Corporation, which housed and educated her but expected her to work for Rockforth as an adult. While displaying a prodigious talent for technology, she also lacked discipline and proved highly irreverent. She was dismissed as a poor investment and now operates a workshop established with her family inheritance.

engineer-hero-ferrari.name: Hero Ferrari

Hero Ferrari

Little is known of Hero's history, and it is widely believed she operates under a pseudonym. She is skilled with weapons modification and demonstrates a keen knowledge of military-grade technology, but appears comfortable in the relatively sedate world of engineering. When not upgrading equipment, Hero practises traditional calligraphy and watercolour techniques.

  • Magazine Size
  • Extra Ammo Capacity
  • Damage Resistance
  • Added Melee Damage
Refers you to
Terra Velasquez
Unlock hand-in
5× Genetic Repair Meds.
In-Game Description
The son of a freedom fighter killed defending his home system, Jude entered military service as soon as he was able, to continue his family's struggle. Years of resistance in an under-equipped militia wore on him until injuries forced him to retire. Jude now puts his engineering talents to use for private clients, but is rumoured to send much of it to family and former comrades.

In-Game Description
Politically conscientious from adolescence, Kit has a reputation for indulging fringe ideologies and conspiracy. An aptitude for engineering makes it easy for him to find work; this income funds an independent broadcast called 'End Times' that serves as a platform for his paranoid rants and apocalyptic predictions.

In-Game Description
From an early age Beck demonstrated a prodigious talent for the sciences, flourishing at the prestigious Dormu Academy and earning several advanced degrees. He briefly worked for the Sirius Corporation, but the relationship did not last, due to a reported difficulty working as part of a team. Using the credits earned from Sirius, Beck established an independent workshop to pursue more personal projects.

In-Game Description
As the only daughter of a prosperous entrepreneur, Terra was born into extraordinary wealth, but a rebellious streak led her to reject the expectations placed upon her. She fled home, falling in with an antiauthoritarian group that engaged in a string of daring robberies. One failed heist and a decade of incarceration later, Terra operates an independent workshop and claims to have left her criminal past behind.

In-Game Description
Hailing from a nation where technology is considered a gateway to spiritual fulfillment, Baltanos serves his church in spreading its transhumanist creed. Many cybernetic implants have altered — or 'blessed' — his ability to manipulate components, facilitating a successful engineering career. Eager to complete a long pilgrimage to humanity's furthest reaches, he now functions as a missionary from his Colonia workshop.

In-Game Description
Born and raised in a starport within a feudalist system, Rosa had few obvious skills in her youth, but had a passion for mechanical things, fascinated by the constant stream of ships outside her apartment window. With donated, swapped and refurbished tools she began to tinker and reverse-engineer the gadgets at home. Her natural talent blossomed into patching up suits and equipment for neighbours, friends, and eventually the whole station.
